title: Schilleria oblonga
intro: Schilleria oblonga is a plant species commonly known as the "Indian Schilleria" or "Indian Sarsaparilla", belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Schilleria oblonga include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schilleria oblonga is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tonics, and ointments.
taxonomy: Schilleria oblonga is a type of plant within the family Celastraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the bittersweet or staff vine family. The Celastraceae family belongs to the order Celastrales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Celastrales is part of the clade asterids, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The family Celastraceae and the order Celastrales are further classified under the clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. At the kingdom level, Schilleria oblonga is classified as a plant within the kingdom Plantae.
